questions.question_text: Answer the following questions CO Mapping a Outline three historical milestones that led to the development of Apache Hadoop. 2.5 b Briefly explain Hadoop’s two‑layer architecture and how it supports scalable data analytics. 2.5 c Explain the role of Flume in data ingestion and contrast it with Sqoop. 2.5 d What is block size in HDFS, and why is setting an appropriate block size important for performance? 2.5 e How does job scheduling in Hadoop ensure cluster resource utilisation? Give one example of a scheduler. 2.5 f Distinguish between key‑value pair formats used in MapReduce jobs. 2.5 g Compare HiveQL tables with traditional RDBMS tables in terms of schema enforcement and query latency. 2.5 h Describe the execution modes of Pig and state one use‑case where each mode is preferable. 2.5
How does Hadoop achieve data locality, and why is it critical for performance? Contrast Hadoop Echo System components with the Hadoop core. 10
Describe the role of YARN in resource management within a Hadoop cluster. Identify two limitations of early Hadoop versions and how modern releases resolve them. 10

subparts.text: Explain how HDFS handles data integrity using checksums.
Explain how Avro supports schema evolution in big-data workflows. 10
Illustrate the write-path in HDFS, covering block replication sequencing.
What is serialization, and why is it important for Hadoop I/O performance? 10

questions.question_text: Describe the purpose of the Combiner and its effect on network traffic. Explain speculative execution and when it may backfire. 10
Discuss how partitioners influence load balancing across reducers. Give two reasons to implement a custom WritableComparable and summarize how to do it. 10
Compare Grunt shell and Hive shell for interactive query prototyping. Describe how user-defined functions extend Pig Latin or HiveQL capabilities. 10
List key differences between HBase and HDFS regarding data access patterns. Explain HBase’s column‑oriented design and architect a write‑heavy, low‑latency time‑series app. 10
